expectations.text: Names SpikeSorting.populate as the immediate next step (not SpikeSortingV1.populate — the actual v1 class is named SpikeSorting)
expectations.evidence: 'the v1 spike sorter class is literally named SpikeSorting (not SpikeSortingV1)'; uses `SpikeSorting.populate(sort_key)`.

expectations.text: Gives an ordered next-N list: SpikeSorting.populate → CurationV1 insert → MetricCuration → SpikeSortingOutput merge insert
expectations.evidence: Flow diagram shows SpikeSorting.populate → CurationV1.insert_curation → MetricCurationSelection→MetricCuration.populate → SpikeSortingOutput.insert.

expectations.text: Notes that CurationV1 comes before MetricCuration in the v1 flow
expectations.evidence: 'must come before MetricCuration, because metric curation computes quality metrics on top of an existing CurationV1 entry'.
